creator: eugene@zhegan.in
text:
Starting from 13.0 PF ignores translation settings for gre(3) (at least !) interfaces.

PF rules:

nat on gre0 inet proto tcp from <mylans> to any -> 10.4.0.42

<mylans> table:

# pfctl -t mylans -T show
   10.8.0.0/24
   172.16.0.1
   192.168.52.0/24
   192.168.53.0/24
   192.168.54.0/24
   192.168.55.0/24
   192.168.56.0/24
   192.168.57.0/24
   192.168.58.0/24
   192.168.59.0/24
   192.168.60.0/24
   192.168.100.0/24
   192.168.175.0/24
   192.168.176.0/24

PF rules with counters:

@3 nat on gre0 inet proto tcp from <mylans:14> to any -> 10.4.0.42
  [ Evaluations: 113076    Packets: 0         Bytes: 0           States: 0     ]
  [ Inserted: uid 0 pid 77972 State Creations: 0     ]

tcpdump on outer interface:

17:03:32.713893 IP 188.17.155.29 > 89.249.17.74: GREv0, length 64: IP 192.168.57.23.50768 > 10.3.51.63.22: Flags [S], seq 384973512, win 29200, options [mss 1460,sackOK,TS val 4036935166 ecr 0,nop,wscale 7], length 0
17:03:33.715044 IP 188.17.155.29 > 89.249.17.74: GREv0, length 64: IP 192.168.57.23.50768 > 10.3.51.63.22: Flags [S], seq 384973512, win 29200, options [mss 1460,sackOK,TS val 4036936168 ecr 0,nop,wscale 7], length 0

Had to use ipfw for that.
This was working across all of the 6.x -> 12.x versions.
